What is the history of CvSU?
responses: Cavite State University (CvSU) traces its roots to 1906 as the Indang Intermediate School, with the American Thomasites as the first teachers. It was renamed Indang Farm School in 1918, Indang Rural High School in 1927, and Don Severino National Agriculture School in 1958. In 1964, the school was converted into a State College via Republic Act 3917 and became known as Don Severino Agricultural College (DSAC). On January 22, 1998, by virtue of Republic Act No. 8468, DSAC was converted into Cavite State University. In 2001, the Cavite College of Fisheries (CACOF) in Naic and the Cavite College of Arts and Trade (CCAT) in Rosario were integrated into the University via CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000. Additional campuses across the province have since been established through memoranda of agreement with local government units. CvSU celebrated its 118th founding anniversary in 2024. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/

CvSU started in 1906 as the Indang Intermediate School (under the American Thomasites). It went through several name changes — Indang Farm School (1918), Indang Rural High School (1927), Don Severino National Agriculture School (1958) — before becoming a State College in 1964 via RA 3917 (Don Severino Agricultural College, DSAC). On January 22, 1998, RA 8468 converted DSAC into Cavite State University. CACOF (Naic) and CCAT (Rosario) joined CvSU in 2001 via CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/

Ang CvSU ay nagsimula noong 1906 bilang Indang Intermediate School, sa panahon ng mga Thomasites. Naging Indang Farm School ito noong 1918, Indang Rural High School noong 1927, Don Severino National Agriculture School noong 1958, at naging State College noong 1964 sa bisa ng RA 3917. Noong Enero 22, 1998, sa bisa ng RA 8468, ito ay naging Cavite State University. Noong 2001, ang CACOF (Naic) at CCAT (Rosario) ay isinama sa CvSU sa pamamagitan ng CHED Memo No. 27 s. 2000. Pinagmulan: https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/

responses: CvSU's tenets are TRUTH, EXCELLENCE, and SERVICE. Per the official Mission, Vision, and Objectives, the University commits to the highest standards of education, values its stakeholders, strives for continual improvement of products and services, and upholds these tenets to produce globally competitive and morally upright individuals. The University's objectives include providing a general education that promotes national identity, cultural consciousness, moral integrity, and spiritual vigor; training the nation's manpower in the skills required by national development; developing professions that will provide leadership for the nation; and advancing knowledge through research work to improve the quality of human life. responses: The CvSU Main Campus — the Don Severino delas Alas Campus — is located in Indang, Cavite, Philippines, approximately 60 km (about 37 miles) southwest of Manila. It is the oldest campus of the University and houses academic, administrative, and research and extension facilities. CvSU operates additional campuses across the province of Cavite, with locations including Bacoor, Cavite City, Carmona, General Trias, Imus, Naic, Rosario (CCAT), Silang, Tanza, and Trece Martires City, among others. For the complete and current campus directory with addresses and contact details, please visit https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/campuses/. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/history/

CvSU has multiple satellite campuses across the province (Bacoor, Cavite City, Carmona, General Trias, Imus, Naic, Rosario/CCAT, Silang, Tanza, Trece Martires City, and others). See https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/campuses/ for the directory with addresses.

responses:
ADMISSION TO CvSU (per the official Office of Student Affairs and Services and the admission portal):

1. FILE THE ONLINE APPLICATION at https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/ — encode your information in the registration/application form.
2. PRINT the accomplished application form on A4-size bond paper.
3. PREPARE THESE DOCUMENTS (typical for SHS-graduate applicants):
   - Certified true copy of Grade 12 Report Card (Form 138)
   - Certificate of Good Moral Character
   - 2 copies of 1x1 ID picture with name tag
   - Short ordinary folder
4. SUBMIT TO OSAS at your target campus for exam scheduling (typically Mondays–Thursdays, 7 AM–6 PM).
5. TAKE THE CvSU ENTRANCE EXAMINATION.
6. APPLICANTS FOR ARCHITECTURE, COMPUTER SCIENCE, AND ENGINEERING must additionally bring their Grade 11 Report Card for evaluation. Engineering programs accept STEM-strand graduates.
7. BS PSYCHOLOGY applicants require a GPA of 85 or higher.
8. TRANSFEREE applicants must have a GPA of 2.00 (equivalent of 85%) or better with NO failed grade.

For the current cycle's exact requirements and deadlines, see https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/ and https://cvsu.edu.ph. Sources: https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/ · https://cvsu.edu.ph/office-of-student-affairs-and-services/

CvSU admission steps: file the online application at admission.cvsu.edu.ph, print the form on A4 bond paper, prepare your Grade 12 Report Card (certified true copy), Good Moral Certificate, 2 copies of 1x1 ID photo with name tag, and a short ordinary folder. Submit to OSAS at your campus for exam scheduling. Architecture/CS/Engineering applicants also bring Grade 11 Report Card (Engineering accepts STEM strand). BS Psychology requires GPA 85+. Transferees: GPA 2.00 (85%) or better, no failed grade. Source: https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/

Mga pangunahing requirements para sa CvSU admission: mag-online application sa admission.cvsu.edu.ph, i-print sa A4 bond paper, ihanda ang certified true copy ng Grade 12 Report Card, Good Moral Certificate, 2 1x1 ID pictures na may name tag, at short ordinary folder. Isumite sa OSAS ng inyong campus para sa exam scheduling. Para sa Architecture/CS/Engineering, magdala rin ng Grade 11 Report Card (Engineering: STEM strand). BS Psychology: GPA 85+. Transferee: GPA 2.00 (85%) at walang failed grade. Pinagmulan: https://admission.cvsu.edu.ph/

responses:
CvSU ENROLLMENT PROCEDURE:

AFTER ADMISSION (NOA received):

1. PRE-ENROLLMENT:
   - Receive Notice of Admission (NOA)
   - Attend department interview/orientation
   - Complete medical examination
   - Get medical clearance

2. ACADEMIC ADVISING:
   - Meet with department adviser
   - Discuss curriculum and course load
   - Get program of study

3. REGISTRATION:
   - Online enrollment via student portal
   - Select courses for the semester
   - Print Certificate of Registration (COR)

4. PAYMENT (Misc fees only - tuition is FREE under RA 10931):
   - Pay miscellaneous fees at Cashier's Office
   - Get official receipt

5. FINALIZATION:
   - Submit COR to Registrar
   - Get student ID
   - Receive class schedule

FOR CONTINUING STUDENTS:
- Online registration through student portal
- Selection of subjects with adviser approval
- Payment of fees if applicable
- COR submission

CONTACT: For the verified Office of the University Registrar contacts (email and phone) for your campus, please check www.cvsu.edu.ph.

CvSU Enrollment Steps: 1) Receive Notice of Admission (NOA) | 2) Attend orientation/interview with your department | 3) Complete medical exam | 4) Meet with your academic adviser for course selection | 5) Register online via the Student Portal | 6) Pay miscellaneous fees at the Cashier (tuition is FREE under RA 10931) | 7) Submit COR to the Registrar | 8) Get your student ID. For continuing students, enroll via the Student Portal during the scheduled period.

Para sa enrollment sa CvSU: tanggapin ang NOA, dumalo sa departmental orientation, kumpletuhin ang medical exam, mag-advising sa iyong adviser, mag-enroll sa Student Portal, bayaran ang miscellaneous fees sa Cashier (libre ang tuition!), at i-submit ang COR sa Registrar. Para sa continuing students, gamitin ang Student Portal sa enrollment period. Salamat sa inyong tanong!

responses:
CvSU TUITION FEES & FREE EDUCATION:

CvSU is covered by RA 10931 (Universal Access to Quality Tertiary Education Act of 2017).

FREE TUITION COVERAGE (For qualified Filipino undergraduate students at SUCs):
- No tuition fee
- Other school fees covered as defined by the law and implementing rules

GENERAL ELIGIBILITY FOR FREE TUITION:
- Filipino citizen
- Passes admission requirements
- Maintains the required academic standing
- Enrolled in an undergraduate degree program
- Has not yet earned a bachelor's degree

WHAT YOU MAY STILL PAY (varies):
- Replacement of school ID or handbook
- Certain optional items (uniform, books)
- Personal expenses (transportation, meals)
- Graduate-school tuition (RA 10931 may not apply to graduate programs)

SCHOLARSHIPS & FINANCIAL AID may include:
- Tertiary Education Subsidy (TES) for living-cost stipends
- Government scholarships
- Private scholarships
- Academic merit scholarships

responses:
CvSU ACADEMIC POLICIES & GRADING SYSTEM:

GRADING SCALE (1.0–5.0):
1.0 = Excellent | 1.25 = Superior | 1.5 = Very Good
1.75 = Good | 2.0 = Satisfactory | 2.25 = Fairly Satisfactory
2.5 = Passing | 2.75 = Conditional Pass | 3.0 = Passed (75%)
5.0 = Failed | INC = Incomplete | W = Withdrawn

MINIMUM PASSING GRADE: 3.0 (75%)

GWA: Weighted average of all course grades × credit units

INCOMPLETE (INC):
- Must be completed within 1 academic year
- Becomes 5.0 (Failed) if not resolved

LEAVE OF ABSENCE (LOA):
- Approved by the Dean; maximum 2 consecutive semesters
- Submit LOA form to the Registrar's Office

SHIFTING:
- Requires Dean's approval; subject to slot availability
- May need to retake non-credited subjects

RETENTION: Varies per college — consult your department for specific GWA requirements.

Contact the Registrar's Office for official grade records and policy clarifications.

At CvSU, the passing grade is 3.0 (equivalent to 75%). Grades run on a 1.0–5.0 scale where 1.0 is Excellent and 5.0 is Failed. GWA is the weighted average of all your grades. An INC (Incomplete) must be resolved within one year or it becomes 5.0. For LOA, shifting, or retention policies, coordinate with your Dean's office and the Registrar.

Ang passing grade sa CvSU ay 3.0 (75%). Gumagamit ang CvSU ng 1.0–5.0 grading scale. Ang GWA ay kinukuha mula sa weighted average ng lahat ng iyong grades. Ang INC grade ay kailangang ayusin sa loob ng isang taon. Para sa LOA, course shifting, o retention policy, makipag-ugnayan sa iyong Dean at sa Registrar's Office. Salamat sa inyong tanong!

CvSU INTERNATIONAL / FOREIGN STUDENT ADMISSIONS:

CvSU accepts foreign nationals subject to CHED and university regulations.

TYPICAL REQUIREMENTS:
- Accomplished application form
- Authenticated/apostilled academic credentials (HS diploma, transcripts)
- Passport copy (biodata page)
- Student Visa (9F) — typically processed AFTER admission
- English-proficiency proof (if from a non-English-speaking country)
- Medical/health clearance
- Police clearance (NBI or equivalent from home country)

TYPICAL PROCESS:
1. Submit application to the Office of Admissions (Main Campus, Indang)
2. Await credential evaluation and Notice of Admission (NOA)
3. Process the 9F Student Visa with the Bureau of Immigration
4. Complete enrollment procedures

TUITION NOTE:
Free tuition under RA 10931 may NOT apply to foreign students. Contact the Cashier's Office for the applicable fees.

FOR VERIFIED CONTACT DETAILS:
Office of Admissions — email: admissions@cvsu.edu.ph
Website: www.cvsu.edu.ph (use the official directory for current phone numbers).

intents.description: Questions about CvSU's academic retention standards, minimum GPA requirements, academic probation, dismissal rules, and how students stay enrolled.
patterns: what GPA do I need to stay in CvSU

Ilang bagsak pwede bago ma-dismiss sa CvSU?
Ano ang retention policy ng CvSU?
What happens if I fail a class at CvSU?
What is the retention policy at CvSU?
How many failing grades before dismissal at CvSU?
responses: CvSU's academic retention standards are governed by the University Student Handbook and the applicable CHED Memorandum Orders for each program. In general, students must maintain a satisfactory General Weighted Average (GWA) and must not accumulate more than the allowed number of failing grades per semester. Students who fall below the required academic standing may be placed on academic probation, given a load restriction, or, in severe cases, recommended for dismissal from the program. Specific GWA cutoffs and the number of allowable failures differ by college and program. I strongly recommend consulting your College's Student Handbook or the Registrar for your program's exact retention rules.

Academic retention at CvSU follows the University Student Handbook provisions on Scholastic Standing. Key points: (1) INC (Incomplete) grades must typically be completed within the prescribed period — unresolved INCs may convert to failing marks; (2) students on academic probation often carry a reduced study load; (3) dismissal from a program may result from repeated failures beyond the threshold set by your college. Maximum residency rules (how many years you may take to finish a degree) also apply per CHED guidelines. Please verify the exact figures with your Registrar or academic adviser, as rules vary by program and year level.

If you're concerned about your academic standing, the first step is to visit your Campus Registrar or your program's academic adviser — they can tell you your current GWA, what the retention cutoff is for your specific program, and whether you are at risk of academic probation or dismissal. CvSU also has an appeals process for students facing academic disqualification; this is handled through the Office of the Registrar and the Dean's Office. Would you like guidance on which office to contact, or do you have a more specific question about grades or program policies?

intents.description: Specific questions about the CvSU grading scale, what grades mean, GWA computation, and how grades affect retention.
patterns: how do failing units affect GWA
responses: CvSU uses a numerical grading scale where 1.0 is the highest (Excellent/Outstanding) and 5.0 is Failing. The common equivalents are: 1.0 = 97–100%, 1.25 = 94–96%, 1.50 = 91–93%, 1.75 = 88–90%, 2.0 = 85–87%, 2.25 = 82–84%, 2.50 = 79–81%, 2.75 = 76–78%, 3.0 = 75% (Passing, minimum), 4.0 = Conditional/Incomplete (INC), 5.0 = Failed. A grade of 3.0 is the minimum passing mark. INC grades must be resolved within the prescribed period; unresolved INCs may become 5.0. Please confirm the exact equivalency table with your Registrar since some programs may use slight variations.

Your General Weighted Average (GWA) at CvSU is computed by multiplying each subject's grade by its unit weight, summing those products, then dividing by total units enrolled. A lower numerical GWA (closer to 1.0) is better. For Latin honors at graduation: Summa Cum Laude typically requires a GWA of 1.20 or better with no failing or dropped subjects; Magna Cum Laude around 1.21–1.45; Cum Laude around 1.46–1.75 — but these thresholds should be verified with the Registrar as they are governed by the Student Handbook and may be updated. Failing subjects (5.0) significantly pull your GWA up (worse) and may trigger academic probation.

The key grades to know at CvSU: 3.0 is the minimum passing grade (equivalent to 75%), and 5.0 is failing. An INC (Incomplete) grade of 4.0 means you have unfinished requirements — it is not failing yet but must be resolved within the period stated in the Student Handbook, or it converts to 5.0. A DRP (Dropped) grade is recorded if you officially withdrew from a subject before the deadline; this differs from being informally absent. Your GWA is affected by all grades and units. For graduation honors or dean's list eligibility, check the exact GWA cutoffs in your campus's Student Handbook or ask the Registrar.

intents.description: How to appeal academic disqualification, petition for retention or reinstatement, and what options exist after being dismissed from a CvSU program.

responses: If you believe a grade was recorded incorrectly or unfairly, you may file a formal grade reconsideration request. The process typically starts with your subject teacher/professor, then escalates to the Department Chair, then the College Dean, and finally to the Registrar if unresolved. Timelines for filing are strict — usually within one semester of the grade being posted — so act quickly. You will likely need to submit a written appeal, any supporting evidence (attendance records, submitted work), and the endorsement of your adviser. Consult your campus Registrar for the exact procedure and deadlines.

For academic dismissal or disqualification, CvSU provides an appeal or petition for retention process through the Office of the Registrar and the Dean's Office of your college. You would typically submit a formal petition letter, your academic records, and supporting documents (medical certificates, financial hardship affidavits, etc. if applicable). The case may be reviewed by an Academic Standing Committee. Some campuses also allow students to shift to a different program rather than face outright dismissal. The Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS) can also provide academic counseling and guidance on your options. Please verify current timelines with your campus Registrar.

Options after academic disqualification at CvSU may include: (1) filing a petition for retention with the Dean's Office, (2) shifting to a different program within CvSU where your academic standing qualifies you, (3) applying for re-admission after a required gap period, or (4) transferring to another institution. Each path has specific requirements and deadlines. The Guidance and Counseling Office at your campus can help you understand your standing and plan next steps. Do not wait — timelines for appeals are usually strictly enforced. Would you like to know more about a specific option?

responses:
RA 10931 — Universal Access to Quality Tertiary Education Act of 2017 — provides free tuition and other school fees (per the law's implementing rules) for qualified Filipino students in undergraduate programs at state universities and colleges, including CvSU.

GENERAL ELIGIBILITY (verify current rules with UniFAST and your CvSU Cashier/OSAS office):
- Filipino citizen
- Admitted to CvSU
- Enrolled in an undergraduate degree program
- Maintains the academic standing required by the institution and the law
- Has not earned a previous undergraduate degree

IMPORTANT NOTES:
- The Tertiary Education Subsidy (TES) is a separate cash subsidy under the same law, processed via UniFAST — priority goes to students from low-income households.
- RA 10931 may not cover graduate programs the same way as undergraduate programs.
- Students may opt out of the free-tuition benefit if they wish.
- Some fees may still apply (replacement IDs, certain personal items, etc.).

For the verified, current implementation at CvSU, please check https://cvsu.edu.ph or contact your campus Cashier/OSAS.

RA 10931 provides free tuition and selected fees for qualified Filipino students in undergraduate programs at SUCs, including CvSU. Eligibility requires Filipino citizenship, admission to an undergraduate program, and maintaining required academic standing. The TES is a separate cash subsidy under the same law. For current implementation details, please contact your campus Cashier or OSAS.

Ang RA 10931 ay nagbibigay ng libreng tuition at iba pang school fees para sa qualified Filipino na undergraduate students sa mga SUC tulad ng CvSU. Kailangan ng Filipino citizenship, admission sa undergraduate program, at pagsunod sa academic standing. Ang TES ay hiwalay na cash subsidy sa ilalim ng parehong batas. Para sa kasalukuyang detalye sa CvSU, makipag-ugnayan sa Cashier o OSAS ng inyong campus.

Graduation at CvSU typically requires: (1) completing all academic units in your curriculum (including any minor/major electives and the NSTP requirement for undergraduate programs), (2) passing the comprehensive exam, thesis/capstone, or practicum required by your program, (3) filing your application as a candidate for graduation through your Dean's Office and the Registrar within the announced window, (4) settling all clearances (library, dormitory if applicable, college, etc.), and (5) paying any graduation-related fees. Exact deadlines, fees, and forms vary per academic year and per campus — please verify with your Dean's Office and the Registrar through https://cvsu.edu.ph.

To graduate from CvSU you need to complete the units in your curriculum, finish program-specific exit requirements (thesis/capstone/practicum/comprehensive exam as applicable), complete NSTP for undergraduate programs, file a graduation application with your Dean and the Registrar in the announced window, and settle all clearances and fees. Deadlines and forms vary per year — please verify with your Dean's Office.

Para mag-graduate sa CvSU, kailangang kumpleto ang units sa inyong kurikulum (kasama ang NSTP), matapos ang thesis/capstone/practicum o comprehensive exam (depende sa programa), maghain ng aplikasyon bilang candidate for graduation sa Dean at Registrar, at ayusin ang lahat ng clearance at fees. responses: CvSU, as a Philippine state university, is governed by the Data Privacy Act of 2012 (RA 10173). Personal data collected from students and staff (e.g., during admission, enrollment, scholarship application, and document requests) is processed only for legitimate university purposes and protected per the law's requirements. As a data subject, you generally have the right to be informed about how your data is used, to access and correct your data, and to file a complaint. To exercise these rights — or to request access, correction, or deletion of your records — please contact the CvSU Data Protection Officer (DPO) through the verified contact channel on https://cvsu.edu.ph.

CvSU complies with the Data Privacy Act of 2012 (RA 10173). You have the right to be informed about how your personal data is processed, to access and correct your records, and to file a complaint. Para sa Data Protection Officer ng CvSU, mangyaring tingnan ang opisyal na contact sa https://cvsu.edu.ph.

CvSU is governed by Philippine laws that protect students from bullying, harassment, and gender-based violence — including the Anti-Bullying Act (RA 10627), the Safe Spaces Act (RA 11313), the Anti-Sexual Harassment Act (RA 7877), and the Anti-VAWC Act (RA 9262). The university has internal policies and committees to receive and act on reports.

IF YOU ARE EXPERIENCING OR HAVE WITNESSED BULLYING / HARASSMENT / GBV at CvSU:
- Document what happened (date, place, people involved, what was said/done) if it is safe to do so.
- Report to the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS), your Guidance Office, or the campus Committee on Decorum and Investigation (CODI) / Gender and Development (GAD) Office — whichever your campus designates as the receiving channel.
- You may also seek support from the Guidance and Counseling Office.

For immediate danger, call 911 or local authorities. For the verified reporting channel at your campus, please refer to https://cvsu.edu.ph.

Adding or dropping a subject at CvSU is allowed only within the Add/Drop Period, which is typically the first 1–2 weeks of the semester (exact dates published per semester). To add: secure approval from your adviser and submit the add form. To officially drop: submit a drop form through your adviser and the Registrar — an officially dropped subject is recorded as DRP, not as a failing grade. Dropping AFTER the deadline without filing may result in a 5.0 (Failed) grade. For the current Add/Drop window, check the academic calendar and your campus Registrar.

Adds and drops are processed during the Add/Drop period at the start of each semester. Officially dropped subjects get a DRP code; unofficial drops or drops after the deadline can become 5.0. Coordinate with your adviser and the Registrar.

Ang add/drop ng subject sa CvSU ay sa loob lamang ng Add/Drop Period sa simula ng semester. Ang opisyal na dropped subject ay tinatakan ng DRP, hindi 5.0. Mag-coordinate sa iyong adviser at Registrar.

patterns: how many subjects can I take
responses: The normal study load at CvSU is the load prescribed in your curriculum for the semester (typically 18–24 units depending on program and year level). Overload (carrying more units than prescribed) usually requires Dean's approval and is most commonly granted to graduating students or those with very strong academic standing. Underload (fewer units than prescribed) may be allowed with adviser approval if you're working, have health concerns, or are catching up on grades — but it may extend your time to graduate. Specific load limits vary per program; consult your adviser and the College Dean.

Normal load is what's prescribed in your curriculum. Overload requires Dean's approval (usually for graduating or top-performing students); underload requires adviser approval. Verify the exact limits with your adviser.

Ang normal na load sa CvSU ay ang nakatakda sa kurikulum (kadalasang 18–24 units). Ang overload ay nangangailangan ng pag-apruba ng Dean. Ang underload ay maaari ngunit posibleng makaapekto sa oras ng graduation. Magtanong sa adviser.

Many CvSU subjects have prerequisites — subjects you must pass first before enrolling in the next one. The prerequisite list is in your curriculum and the course catalog of your program. In most cases, taking a subject without its prerequisite is not allowed; petitions or waivers may be considered by the Dean only in special circumstances. Co-requisites are subjects that must be taken together (or one must be taken first). Always check prerequisites before enrolling — the Registrar's system normally enforces them.
Prerequisites are enforced — you generally can't enroll in a subject without having passed its prereq. Waivers are rare and require Dean approval. Check the curriculum or ask your adviser.
May mga subject sa CvSU na may prerequisite — kailangan munang pumasa sa isa bago kumuha ng kasunod. Tingnan ang inyong kurikulum o magtanong sa adviser.

A Summer Term is offered by CvSU when there is sufficient demand for a subject and faculty availability — typically used to retake failed subjects, advance in a curriculum, or catch up after an LOA. Summer offerings vary per campus and per program; not all subjects are offered every summer. Summer enrollment usually opens after the second semester ends. Note: tuition coverage under RA 10931 generally applies to summer for qualified undergraduate students, but verify with the Cashier's Office. Check https://cvsu.edu.ph for current summer offerings.

Summer Term offerings at CvSU vary per campus and per program. Used commonly for retakes or to advance. Enrollment opens after the second semester. For current summer offerings, check with your Dean's Office or https://cvsu.edu.ph.
Mayroong Summer Term sa CvSU kapag may sapat na demand at faculty availability. Karaniwang ginagamit ito para sa retake o pagkadami ng units. Ang summer offerings ay nag-iiba bawat campus.

Ladderized education in the Philippines is governed by RA 10647 (Ladderized Education Act of 2014), which allows TESDA-issued National Certificates (NC I, II, III) and CHED-recognized undergraduate units to ladder into each other under qualified programs. Some CvSU programs participate in ladderized arrangements, allowing students to exit with a certificate or AS/AAS credential and re-enter to complete a bachelor's degree later. Availability is program-specific — verify which CvSU programs are ladderized with the Office of Academic Affairs through https://cvsu.edu.ph.

RA 10647 enables ladderized education between TESDA NCs and CHED-aligned bachelor's programs. CvSU participates in select ladderized programs — verify availability with the Office of Academic Affairs.
Ang ladderized education (RA 10647) ay nagpapahintulot ng pagdaloy mula TESDA papunta sa bachelor's degree. May ilang programa sa CvSU na ladderized — kumpirmahin sa Office of Academic Affairs.

patterns: what strand for engineering at CvSU
what strand for IT at CvSU
responses: CvSU generally welcomes graduates of any Senior High School (SHS) strand for admission, but the strand you finished can influence how well-prepared you are for a specific program and, in some cases, whether you need bridging subjects. For example, STEM aligns naturally with engineering, IT, computer science, and the health sciences; ABM aligns with business and accountancy; HUMSS aligns with education, social sciences, and communication; TVL with technology and trade programs. You can still cross-apply from any strand — academic readiness is assessed via the entrance exam and the receiving college's evaluation. Specific bridging requirements vary per program.

Any SHS strand can apply to CvSU programs, but alignment may simplify your transition. STEM → engineering/IT/health, ABM → business, HUMSS → education/social sciences/communication, TVL → technology/trades. Cross-strand applicants may need bridging subjects depending on the program.

Tumatanggap ang CvSU ng kahit anong SHS strand para sa admission. Maaaring magkaroon ng bridging subjects depende sa programa. Ang STEM ay angkop para sa engineering/IT, ABM sa business, HUMSS sa edukasyon/social sciences, at TVL sa technology/trade.

patterns: tanggap ba ang ALS sa CvSU
responses: Yes, CvSU accepts ALS (Alternative Learning System) Accreditation & Equivalency (A&E) passers. As an ALS applicant you generally submit your ALS Certificate of Rating / Diploma in lieu of Form 138, plus the other standard freshman documents (PSA birth certificate, Good Moral, 2x2 photos, application form). You take the same CvSU entrance examination and are evaluated together with other applicants. For the current process and any additional requirements for ALS applicants, please confirm with the Admissions Office through https://cvsu.edu.ph.
CvSU accepts ALS A&E passers. Submit your ALS Certificate of Rating in place of Form 138, plus the standard freshman documents. You'll take the same entrance exam.
Tumatanggap ang CvSU ng ALS A&E passer. Ipakita ang inyong ALS Certificate of Rating bilang katumbas ng Form 138, kasama ang ibang standard na requirements. Kukuha rin kayo ng CvSU entrance exam.

Homeschooled applicants are generally admitted to CvSU if they hold a recognized DepEd Senior High School completion certificate or its equivalent (e.g., through a DepEd-accredited homeschool provider, or through the ALS A&E pathway). You'll typically submit the equivalent of Form 138 (issued by your accredited provider or DepEd), and the standard freshman documents. The CvSU entrance examination still applies. For the current requirements specific to homeschooled applicants, please verify with the Admissions Office through https://cvsu.edu.ph.

Homeschooled applicants are accepted at CvSU if their credentials are DepEd-recognized (accredited homeschool provider or ALS pathway). The standard entrance exam and freshman requirements still apply. Confirm details with Admissions.

Tumatanggap ang CvSU ng homeschooled applicants kung ang kanilang credentials ay kilala ng DepEd. Karaniwang ginagamit ang katumbas ng Form 138 mula sa accredited provider. Magtanong sa Admissions Office sa https://cvsu.edu.ph.

Common enrollment problems and where to go:
- Account or portal login issue → ICT/MIS Office at your campus
- Subjects not appearing or unable to select → your adviser, then the Registrar
- Section full / no slots → coordinate with your Dean's Office; petition for a class may be allowed if enough students are affected
- Schedule conflict between subjects → adjust with your adviser; one or both may need to be moved or dropped
- COR not generating after subject selection → Registrar's Office
- Holds on your record (unpaid balance, unreturned books, missing clearance) → settle with the relevant office (Cashier, Library, etc.)
If you're stuck, your first stop is usually your academic adviser, then the Registrar.

If you can't enroll: log in issues → ICT/MIS; subject/COR issues → Registrar; section full → Dean's Office; account holds → settle with the office that issued them (Cashier, Library, etc.). Start with your adviser if you're not sure which office to approach.

Para sa mga problema sa enrollment: login issues → ICT/MIS; problema sa subject o COR → Registrar; punong section → Dean's Office; account holds → ayusin sa kaukulang opisina (Cashier, Library, atbp.). Magsimula sa inyong adviser kung hindi sigurado.

Credit transfer / accreditation of subjects from a previous school or a cross-enrollment is processed by the receiving CvSU college after you are officially admitted/enrolled. The procedure typically: submit your previous TOR and the syllabi of the subjects you wish to credit, the college evaluates equivalency against CvSU's curriculum, and approved units are reflected in your CvSU record. Not all subjects credit — equivalency depends on content match, credit hours, and CHED guidelines. Coordinate with your Dean's Office for the form and timeline.

Credit transfer is evaluated by your receiving college after admission. Submit previous TOR and course syllabi; equivalency depends on content match and credit hours. Coordinate with your Dean's Office.

Ang credit transfer ay sinusuri ng tatanggap na college pagkatapos ng admission. Magdala ng TOR at syllabi ng mga subject. Ang equivalency ay depende sa nilalaman at credit hours. Magtanong sa Dean's Office.

Most CvSU undergraduate programs require a thesis, capstone project, or culminating activity (the form varies by program — e.g., research thesis in science programs, capstone software project for IT/CS, business plan for entrepreneurship). The general flow: form a group (if required), choose a topic and adviser, defend a proposal, conduct the study/build the project, defend the final manuscript before a panel, revise as required, and submit the bound copy to the library/department. The exact format manual, deadlines, and committee composition vary per college; consult your department's thesis/capstone handbook.

Most CvSU programs require a thesis or capstone project. The flow: topic + adviser → proposal defense → execution → final defense → revisions → submission. Exact format and timelines vary per department.

Karamihan ng CvSU undergraduate programs ay may thesis o capstone. Ang flow: topic + adviser → proposal defense → ehekusyon → final defense → revisions → submission. Ang format at timelines ay depende sa departamento.

A comprehensive examination is commonly required for graduate students (Master's, Doctoral) and sometimes for certain undergraduate programs as a culminating evaluation. It typically covers major subjects in your program and is administered by a department panel. Schedule, coverage, passing mark, and number of allowed retakes vary by program. Coordinate with your department's Graduate Coordinator (for graduate programs) or your College Dean for the current rules.

Comprehensive exams are typically required for graduate students and some undergraduate programs. Coverage, schedule, and retake rules vary by program. Coordinate with your department or Graduate Coordinator.

Ang comprehensive examination ay karaniwang kailangan sa graduate programs at sa ilang undergraduate programs. Ang sakop at retake rules ay depende sa programa. Magtanong sa Department/Graduate Coordinator.

A removal examination (commonly called 'removal') may be offered at the end of the semester to students with a conditional grade or who failed a specific subject under defined circumstances — but availability is at the discretion of the instructor and the department, and not all subjects permit one. The procedure and fee are usually set per department. If a removal is not available or you fail it, the standard path is to retake the subject in a future semester. Confirm with your subject teacher and Department Chair.

Removal exams may be allowed at end-of-semester for conditional/failed grades — availability varies per subject and instructor. If unavailable or if you fail it, retake the subject. Confirm with your Department Chair.
Ang removal exam ay maaaring iaalok sa katapusan ng semester para sa conditional/failed grade — ngunit hindi lahat ng subject ay may removal. Magtanong sa instructor at Department Chair.

A special class (sometimes called a 'tutorial', 'petition class', or 'independent study') may be allowed at CvSU when a subject is not offered in the current semester but a small number of students need it (often graduating students or those affected by curriculum changes). The procedure typically requires: a petition signed by the affected students (a minimum number applies), a willing faculty member, approval of the Department Chair and the College Dean, and any applicable fees. Approval is not guaranteed. Coordinate with your Department Chair early.

Special/petition classes are allowed when a subject isn't offered but several students need it. Requires a faculty member willing to teach, a minimum number of petitioners, and Department/Dean approval.

Maaaring magkaroon ng special/petition class kung hindi inaalok ang isang subject. Kailangan ng faculty na handa, minimum na bilang ng nag-pe-petition, at pag-apruba ng Department at Dean. Magtanong sa Department Chair.

Auditing a subject (attending classes without earning credit) may be allowed at CvSU at the instructor's discretion and with the approval of the Dean. Audit students typically don't take exams or receive a grade, and the subject doesn't count toward graduation. Specific rules — including any fees and whether your status (current student, alumnus, non-student) qualifies — vary per campus. Inquire with the Department Chair and the Registrar.
Auditing classes (no credit, no grade) is at the instructor and Dean's discretion. Fees and eligibility vary — verify with the Department and Registrar.
Ang pag-audit ng subject (walang credit, walang grado) ay maaari ngunit nasa pag-apruba ng instructor at Dean. Magtanong sa Department at Registrar.

An INC (Incomplete) grade at CvSU means the subject's requirements are not yet fully met. To remove it, you must complete the deficient requirement (project, exam, paper) with your subject teacher within the prescribed period — usually within one academic year. Once the teacher submits the completion to the Registrar, your INC is replaced with the final grade. If you fail to complete within the prescribed period, the INC typically converts to 5.0 (Failed). Coordinate directly with the subject teacher and verify deadlines with the Registrar.

INC means an unfulfilled requirement. Complete it with the teacher within the prescribed period (usually one year), and the teacher submits the corrected grade to the Registrar. Unresolved INCs may become 5.0.
Ang INC ay kulang na requirement. Tapusin ito sa loob ng prescribed period (kadalasang isang taon) at i-submit ng instructor sa Registrar ang final grade. Kung hindi natapos, maaaring maging 5.0.

CvSU honors are governed by the University Student Handbook. The two main categories are:
- DEAN'S LIST — semestral recognition for students who meet a high-GWA standard with no failing/INC/DRP grades and a minimum study load.
- LATIN HONORS — at graduation: Cum Laude, Magna Cum Laude, and Summa Cum Laude, awarded based on cumulative GWA with strict additional conditions (no failing grades, no dropped subjects, minimum residency at CvSU, no academic dishonesty, etc.).
Exact GWA thresholds and conditions vary and are set in the Student Handbook. Verify the current cutoffs with the Registrar.

Honors at CvSU: Dean's List (semestral, high GWA + no failing grades + minimum load) and Latin Honors at graduation (Cum/Magna/Summa, by cumulative GWA + strict conditions). Exact thresholds in the Student Handbook — verify with the Registrar.
Mga honors sa CvSU: Dean's List (per semester) at Latin Honors sa graduation (Cum, Magna, Summa). May mga GWA thresholds at strict conditions. Tingnan ang Student Handbook o magtanong sa Registrar.

Academic integrity at CvSU is governed by the University Student Handbook and applicable codes of conduct. Violations include plagiarism, cheating in examinations, contract cheating/ghostwriting, fabrication of data, and unauthorized collaboration. Penalties range from a failing grade in the specific requirement, a failing grade in the subject, to suspension or dismissal for serious or repeated offenses. Reports are typically reviewed by the College Dean and, depending on severity, by a Student Discipline Committee. Always cite sources properly and follow the prescribed citation style of your program.

Plagiarism and cheating at CvSU carry penalties ranging from a failing grade in a requirement to dismissal for serious cases. Review the Student Handbook and follow your program's citation requirements.

Ang academic dishonesty (plagiarism, cheating) sa CvSU ay may parusang nag-uumpisa sa failing grade hanggang dismissal sa malalang kaso. Tingnan ang Student Handbook at gamitin nang tama ang citation.

Research involving human or animal subjects at CvSU is reviewed by the appropriate research ethics committee (IRB or its equivalent) to ensure compliance with national rules (PHREB / DOST-PCHRD guidelines, the Data Privacy Act of 2012, the Animal Welfare Act, etc.). Typical requirements include: research proposal, data privacy plan, informed consent forms, and adviser endorsement. Submit through your College Research Office or the University Research Office. For the current ethics clearance process and forms, please contact the research office at your campus.

Human/animal subjects research at CvSU goes through ethics review (IRB or equivalent), with national rules from PHREB, the Data Privacy Act, and Animal Welfare Act applying. Coordinate through your College Research Office.
Ang research na may human o animal subjects sa CvSU ay sumasailalim sa ethics review batay sa PHREB, Data Privacy Act, at Animal Welfare Act. Mag-coordinate sa College Research Office.

patterns: I think my grade is wrong
responses: If you believe a grade was recorded incorrectly or was unfair, the standard escalation path at CvSU is: (1) raise the concern with your subject teacher first — minor errors can be fixed by a change-of-grade form filed by the instructor; (2) if unresolved, escalate to the Department Chair; (3) then to the College Dean; (4) finally, to the Registrar if the issue is documentary. Timelines for grade disputes are strict — usually within one semester of the grade being posted. Bring evidence (graded papers, attendance records, submitted work).
Grade disputes escalate teacher → Department Chair → Dean → Registrar. Strict timelines apply (usually within one semester of posting). Bring evidence to support your claim.
Para sa grade dispute: instructor → Department Chair → Dean → Registrar. Mahigpit ang deadline (kadalasang isang semester mula sa pag-post). Magdala ng ebidensya.

CvSU offers a range of agriculture-related undergraduate programs through the College of Agriculture, Food, Environment and Natural Resources (or its current equivalent name) — typically including BS Agriculture (with specializations), BS Agricultural and Biosystems Engineering, BS Animal Science, BS Food Technology, BS Forestry/Agroforestry, BS Agribusiness Management, and related programs. Availability varies per campus. For the current program list and the campuses that offer each, please check https://cvsu.edu.ph.

CvSU's agriculture cluster typically offers BS Agriculture (with specializations), BS Agricultural Engineering, BS Animal Science, BS Food Technology, BS Agroforestry, and BS Agribusiness. Availability varies per campus.
Ang CvSU ay nag-aalok ng mga agriculture-related programs: BS Agriculture, BS Agricultural Engineering, BS Animal Science, BS Food Technology, atbp. Ang availability ay nag-iiba bawat campus.

Engineering at CvSU is housed in the College of Engineering and Information Technology (CEIT). Per the official CEIT Academic Programs page, the college's offerings include engineering programs alongside BS Computer Science and BS Information Technology. Engineering programs at CvSU accept STEM-strand SHS graduates and require submission of the Grade 11 Report Card for program evaluation during admission. For the complete and current list of engineering programs (by degree and campus), please refer to https://cvsu.edu.ph/2018/01/13/ceit-academic-programs/ and https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/.

Engineering programs at CvSU sit within CEIT (College of Engineering and Information Technology). They accept STEM-strand SHS graduates, and Grade 11 Report Card is required at admission for program evaluation. For the current list of engineering programs and campuses, see https://cvsu.edu.ph/2018/01/13/ceit-academic-programs/

Ang mga engineering programs ng CvSU ay nasa CEIT (College of Engineering and Information Technology). Tumatanggap ito ng STEM-strand SHS graduates at kailangan ng Grade 11 Report Card sa admission. Para sa kasalukuyang listahan ng programa, tingnan ang https://cvsu.edu.ph/2018/01/13/ceit-academic-programs/.

CvSU offers teacher-education programs commonly including: Bachelor of Elementary Education (BEEd) and Bachelor of Secondary Education (BSEd) with majors in English, Mathematics, Science, Filipino, Social Studies, TLE, and similar fields. Bachelor of Special Needs Education (BSNEd) and Bachelor of Early Childhood Education (BECEd) may also be offered at certain campuses. Graduates take the Licensure Examination for Teachers (LET) administered by the PRC. Program availability and majors vary per campus.
CvSU offers BEEd and BSEd (with majors) and may offer BSNEd and BECEd at certain campuses. Graduates take the PRC LET to become licensed teachers.
Ang CvSU ay nag-aalok ng BEEd at BSEd (na may iba't ibang mejor) at maaaring BSNEd at BECEd sa ilang campus. Ang mga graduates ay kumukuha ng LET.

CvSU's College of Nursing offers BS Nursing, BS Medical Technology, and Diploma in Midwifery (per the official Colleges listing). Availability and slots may vary per campus and academic year. For the current admission and program details, see https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/ and the College of Nursing's official page.
The College of Nursing at CvSU offers BS Nursing, BS Medical Technology, and Diploma in Midwifery. See https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/ for the latest details.
Ang College of Nursing ng CvSU ay nag-aalok ng BS Nursing, BS Medical Technology, at Diploma in Midwifery. Para sa kasalukuyang detalye, tingnan ang https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/.

patterns: BS Animal Science to Vet Med
responses: Veterinary education at CvSU is offered through the College of Veterinary Medicine and Biomedical Sciences. Admission to the Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) program typically requires completion of pre-veterinary undergraduate prerequisites and is competitive. Graduates take the Veterinary Medicine Licensure Examination administered by the PRC. For the current admission process, prerequisites, and program details, please refer to the college's page via https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/.

CvSU offers Veterinary Medicine through the College of Veterinary Medicine and Biomedical Sciences. DVM admission requires pre-vet prerequisites. Graduates take the PRC Veterinary licensure exam. Details: https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/.

Ang Veterinary Medicine sa CvSU ay sa ilalim ng College of Veterinary Medicine and Biomedical Sciences. Ang admission sa DVM ay nangangailangan ng pre-vet prerequisites; ang graduates ay kumukuha ng PRC Veterinary licensure exam. Tingnan ang https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/.

CvSU's hospitality and tourism programs sit under the College of Tourism and Hospitality Management (CTHM), which the Board of Regents approved as an independent college on September 26, 2024. CTHM offers BS Hospitality Management and BS Tourism Management. Business and accountancy programs (e.g., BSBA, BS Accountancy, BS Entrepreneurship) are offered at the Main Campus and selected satellite campuses through their respective colleges/departments. For the current list of business/management programs and the campuses that offer each, see https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/.

CTHM (College of Tourism and Hospitality Management) — approved as an independent college on 26 Sept 2024 — offers BS Hospitality Management and BS Tourism Management. Business/accountancy programs are offered at the Main Campus and selected satellite campuses. See https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/ for the current list. Source: https://cvsu.edu.ph/college-of-tourism-and-hospitality-management/

Ang CTHM (College of Tourism and Hospitality Management) — inaprubahan bilang independent college noong Sept 26, 2024 — ay nag-aalok ng BS Hospitality Management at BS Tourism Management. Ang business/accountancy programs ay sa Main Campus at sa ilang satellite campus. Tingnan ang https://cvsu.edu.ph/category/colleges/. Pinagmulan: https://cvsu.edu.ph/college-of-tourism-and-hospitality-management/

Bachelor of Science in Criminology may be offered at select CvSU campuses subject to CHED program permits and accreditation. The program prepares graduates for the Criminologist Licensure Examination administered by the PRC. Availability and admission requirements vary per campus and per year. For the current list of campuses offering BS Criminology and the verified requirements, please check https://cvsu.edu.ph.
BS Criminology may be offered at select CvSU campuses. Graduates take the PRC Criminologist Licensure Examination. Availability varies — verify via https://cvsu.edu.ph.
Maaaring inaalok ang BS Criminology sa ilang CvSU campus. Ang mga graduates ay kumukuha ng PRC Criminologist Licensure Exam. Kumpirmahin sa https://cvsu.edu.ph.

To request a Transcript of Records (TOR) from CvSU: (1) file the request at the Office of the University Registrar (online or in person, depending on what your campus supports), (2) pay the applicable fee at the Cashier, (3) attach any clearance required, (4) claim the document or have it mailed if that option is available. Common uses (board exam, employment, transfer, embassy) each may require a different number of copies and additional authentication (CAV for CHED, DFA apostille for foreign use, red ribbon for older documents). Processing time and fees vary — verify with the Registrar at your campus.

TOR request: file at the Registrar, pay at the Cashier, attach clearance, claim or have it mailed. Different uses (board, work, transfer, embassy) may need different authentication (CAV, DFA apostille). Verify fees and processing time with the Registrar.

Para sa TOR request: mag-file sa Registrar, magbayad sa Cashier, isumite ang clearance, at kunin o ipadala. Iba't ibang gamit (board exam, trabaho, transfer, embahada) ay maaaring kailangan ng iba't ibang authentication. Magtanong sa Registrar.

patterns: when do I get my diploma
responses: Your CvSU diploma is typically released after graduation, once the Registrar has finalized all records and the Special Order (SO) from CHED has been received (where applicable). To claim: present a valid ID and your graduation clearance. Lost diploma → request a replacement or Certified True Copy (CTC) from the Registrar; you'll typically need a notarized Affidavit of Loss and pay applicable fees. Processing time and the exact fee schedule vary — verify with the Registrar.

Diplomas are released after Registrar finalizes records and (where applicable) the CHED Special Order arrives. Lost diploma → notarized Affidavit of Loss + request CTC/replacement from the Registrar. Fees and timing vary.

Ang diploma sa CvSU ay ibinibigay pagkatapos ma-finalize ng Registrar ang records at madatnan ang CHED Special Order. Para sa nawalang diploma — kailangan ng Affidavit of Loss at request ng CTC sa Registrar.

patterns: need good moral for board exam
responses: A Certificate of Good Moral Character from CvSU is issued by the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS), or in some campuses by the Guidance Office or Registrar, depending on internal procedure. To request: fill out the form, pay any applicable fee at the Cashier, and claim per the published schedule. Commonly used for transferring to another school, applying for board exams, and employment. Processing time varies — verify with your campus OSAS/Registrar.
Good Moral certificates are issued by OSAS (or the Registrar/Guidance Office depending on campus). File request, pay fee, claim. Used for transfer, board exam, and employment.

Ang Certificate of Good Moral Character ay ibinibigay ng OSAS (o Registrar/Guidance Office). Mag-file ng request, magbayad ng bayad, at kunin. Karaniwang ginagamit sa transfer, board exam, at trabaho.

If you need CvSU documents authenticated for use abroad, the typical path is: (1) request the document (TOR / Diploma / Good Moral) from the CvSU Registrar; (2) obtain a Certification, Authentication, and Verification (CAV) from CHED for tertiary documents; (3) apostille the authenticated document at the DFA (the apostille has replaced the old 'red ribbon' under the Apostille Convention since 2019). Processing fees and times apply at each step. Plan ahead — the full chain can take weeks. For the current CHED/DFA process, check the agencies' official websites; for CvSU document fees, check with the Registrar.
Documents-for-abroad chain: CvSU Registrar → CHED CAV → DFA apostille. Apostille replaced the old red ribbon in 2019. Plan ahead — the full chain can take weeks.
Para sa dokumentong gagamitin sa ibang bansa: CvSU Registrar → CHED CAV → DFA apostille. Ang apostille ang pumalit sa 'red ribbon' simula 2019. Magbalak nang maaga.

To correct a name or other personal data in your CvSU records: file a written request at the Registrar's Office, attach supporting documents (e.g., PSA Birth Certificate, Marriage Certificate, court order for change of name, valid IDs), and pay any applicable fees. Corrections to issued documents (TOR, Diploma) may require additional steps and processing time. Some corrections — particularly to a printed diploma — are not always possible after release; verify the procedure with the Registrar.

Name/data corrections in CvSU records require a written request at the Registrar with PSA-issued supporting documents. Corrections to issued diplomas may have additional rules — verify with the Registrar.
Para sa pagwawasto ng pangalan sa CvSU records: mag-file sa Registrar na may PSA documents at IDs. Ang pagwawasto sa nailabas na diploma ay maaaring may karagdagang proseso — magtanong sa Registrar.

CvSU has varsity teams that compete in inter-collegiate and inter-university tournaments, including the State Colleges and Universities Athletic Association (SCUAA) Region IV-A and national qualifiers. Common varsity sports include basketball, volleyball, football, athletics (track & field), and others. Try-outs are typically held before each season and announced through the Sports Director's Office and Student Affairs. Athletic scholarships and stipends may be available for selected varsity athletes — terms vary. Check with your campus Sports Office for the current try-out schedule.

CvSU varsity teams compete in SCUAA and other tournaments. Try-outs happen before each season — check with your campus Sports Office for the current schedule. Athletic scholarships may be available for select varsity athletes.

May mga varsity team ang CvSU na sumasali sa SCUAA at iba pang tournament. May try-outs bago bawat season — magtanong sa Sports Office ng inyong campus. May athletic scholarship sa ilang varsity athletes.

Intramurals (Sportsfest) at CvSU is the annual university or campus-level competition pitting colleges/departments against each other in sports, cheering, cultural performances, and academic events. Dates are set each academic year by Student Affairs and the Sports Office. Participation is typically free for enrolled students — try-outs and team selection are handled at the college/department level. For the current year's schedule and event list, follow CvSU's verified Facebook page and your campus Student Affairs office.
Intramurals at CvSU is an annual college-vs-college sports and culture festival. Dates set per year; team selection at the college/department level. Follow OSAS announcements for the schedule.
Ang Intramurals/Sportsfest sa CvSU ay taunang labanan ng mga college/department sa sports, cheer, at kultura. Itinatakda ang petsa bawat taon. Sumunod sa anunsiyo ng OSAS at Sports Office.

The Supreme Student Government (SSG) is the umbrella student government of CvSU, with a university-wide body and college/department-level councils. SSG elections are typically held once each academic year, with COMELEC-equivalent oversight by Student Affairs. To run, you generally need to be a bona fide enrolled student, meet academic and conduct requirements set by the election code, and follow the filing/campaign rules announced during election season. For this year's election dates and the SSG constitution/by-laws, check with OSAS at your campus.

The SSG is CvSU's umbrella student government, with annual elections overseen by Student Affairs. Eligibility and filing rules are set by the SSG election code. Check OSAS for this year's election dates.

Ang Supreme Student Government (SSG) ang student government ng CvSU. May taunang halalan na pinangangasiwaan ng Student Affairs. Sumangguni sa OSAS para sa eligibility, deadlines, at petsa ng halalan.

CvSU campuses operate official student publications (the Main Campus's paper is The Gazette, with other campuses having their own publications). They typically recruit through writing tryouts/screening tests each academic year for staff writers, photojournalists, cartoonists, layout artists, and copy editors. Membership rules and recruitment timing vary per publication. To join, attend the publication's open call announcement (usually early in the semester) or visit their office at your campus.
Student publications at CvSU (e.g., The Gazette at Main Campus) recruit through writing tryouts each year. Watch for the open-call announcement or visit the publication office at your campus.
Ang student publications ng CvSU (tulad ng The Gazette sa Main Campus) ay may taunang tryouts. Sumunod sa open-call announcement o pumunta sa publication office ng inyong campus.

CvSU has student organizations across various faith traditions — typically including Catholic groups, evangelical/Christian fellowships, and others, depending on campus. The Main Campus and some others have a chapel; prayer rooms may be available at other campuses. Mass and worship schedules are set by the resident chaplain or partner ministries and posted at the chapel. For the list of recognized religious organizations at your campus, contact OSAS.
Faith-based student organizations are present at CvSU (Catholic groups, Christian fellowships, and others, depending on campus). For schedules and the recognized-org list, contact your campus OSAS.
May iba't ibang faith-based student organizations sa CvSU. Para sa iskedyul ng misa o worship at listahan ng recognized religious orgs, makipag-ugnayan sa OSAS.

CvSU is committed to a respectful and inclusive learning environment for all students, including LGBTQ+ students. Discrimination, harassment, or bullying based on Sexual Orientation, Gender Identity and Expression (SOGIE) is prohibited under the Safe Spaces Act (RA 11313) and the Anti-Bullying Act (RA 10627). If you experience or witness discrimination, you can report to the Office of Student Affairs and Services (OSAS), Guidance Office, or the campus Gender and Development (GAD) Office. For policies on preferred name, pronouns, and other gender-related student services, please coordinate with OSAS and the GAD Office at your campus.
CvSU prohibits SOGIE-based discrimination under the Safe Spaces Act and Anti-Bullying Act. Report concerns to OSAS, Guidance, or the campus GAD Office.
Ipinagbabawal ng CvSU ang diskriminasyon batay sa SOGIE alinsunod sa Safe Spaces Act at Anti-Bullying Act. Maaaring iulat ang anumang isyu sa OSAS, Guidance, o sa GAD Office ng inyong campus.

CvSU follows national laws on access for persons with disabilities, including the Magna Carta for Persons with Disabilities (RA 7277, as amended). Students with disabilities are entitled to reasonable accommodations for academic activities and assessments. Common services include ramp/lift access (where infrastructure permits), priority seating, exam accommodations (extended time, scribe, accessible format), and coordination with the Guidance/OSAS office. To request accommodations, contact the OSAS / Guidance Office and your College Dean as early as possible — preferably at enrollment.
PWD students at CvSU are entitled to reasonable accommodations under RA 7277. Request via OSAS/Guidance and your Dean as early as possible (ideally at enrollment).
Ang mga PWD students ay may karapatan sa reasonable accommodations sa CvSU sa ilalim ng RA 7277. Magrequest sa OSAS/Guidance at sa Dean nang maaga, idealmente sa enrollment.

Public transport routes to CvSU vary by destination campus. For the Main Campus in Indang, common options include jeepneys/UV vans from nearby town terminals (e.g., from Dasmariñas, Trece Martires, or Tagaytay) into Indang town proper, then a tricycle to the campus gate. Other CvSU campuses have local terminals served by jeepneys and buses. Travel time and fare depend on traffic and route. For up-to-date local route information, ask current CvSU students at your target campus or check current commuting forums — bus/jeepney route schedules change.

Public transport to the Main Campus (Indang) typically goes via jeepneys/UV vans from Dasmariñas, Trece Martires, or Tagaytay, then tricycle to the gate. For other campuses, local terminals serve them. Route schedules change — verify locally.

Para sa Main Campus sa Indang, karaniwang sumasakay ng jeep/UV mula Dasmariñas, Trece, o Tagaytay, tapos tricycle papasok sa campus. Para sa ibang campus, may lokal na terminals. Magbago ang ruta — tanungin nang lokal.

Parking on CvSU campuses is typically allowed for students, faculty, and staff in designated lots — usually with a campus parking sticker/pass issued by Security or the Administrative Services office. Motorcycle and car parking areas are separate. Rules, sticker fees, and pass procedures vary per campus. For the current parking-pass procedure at your campus, please contact your campus Security/Administrative office.
Parking on CvSU campuses uses designated lots with a parking sticker/pass issued by Security or Admin Services. Rules and fees vary per campus.
May designated parking sa mga CvSU campus, na may parking sticker/pass mula sa Security o Admin Services. Iba-iba ang patakaran at bayad bawat campus.

Most CvSU campuses have a canteen / cafeteria and student-accessible food stalls offering affordable meals. Outside the gates, additional carinderias and small eateries usually serve students. Operating hours typically align with class hours. Specific options (vegetarian, halal, food-allergy-safe meals) vary per campus and vendor — ask the vendor directly when ordering. For specific dietary needs, you may also coordinate with the Health Service Unit and Guidance Office if relevant.
CvSU campuses typically have canteens and food stalls inside, plus carinderias outside the gates. Options for vegetarian/halal/allergen-safe meals vary per vendor — ask directly.
Karaniwang may canteen at food stalls sa loob ng CvSU campuses, at mga carinderia sa labas. Ang vegetarian, halal, o allergen-safe na pagkain ay nag-iiba bawat vendor — magtanong nang direkta.

patterns: mabilis ba ang wifi sa CvSU
responses: Wi-Fi is typically available at CvSU campuses in libraries, computer laboratories, and certain common areas. Access usually requires a student account or campus-issued credentials; the ICT/MIS Office at your campus issues these. Coverage, speed, and the specific SSIDs available vary per campus and per area. For Wi-Fi access requests, password resets, or coverage issues, please contact the ICT/MIS Office.
Wi-Fi is available at CvSU in libraries, computer labs, and common areas. Access usually requires campus credentials from ICT/MIS. Coverage and speed vary — report issues to ICT/MIS.
May Wi-Fi sa CvSU sa mga library, computer lab, at common areas. Karaniwang kailangan ng campus credentials mula sa ICT/MIS. Magreport ng issues sa ICT/MIS.

Printing, photocopying, scanning, and binding services are typically available at the campus library and at private shops just outside campus gates — these are operated by third parties, so rates and turnaround vary. For thesis printing and binding, allow extra time around graduation rush. There is no university-wide standard rate that I can verify.
Printing/photocopy/scan/binding services are usually available at the library and outside-gate shops. Rates and turnaround vary by provider — especially during graduation rush.
Karaniwang may printing, photocopy, scan, at binding sa library at sa labas ng gate ng CvSU. Ang presyo at oras ng paghahanda ay nag-iiba bawat tindahan.

Many CvSU campuses have a small student store or coop selling school supplies, and accredited tailors/uniform suppliers near campus for departmental uniforms (PE, NSTP, lab gowns, engineering aprons, etc.). For textbooks, the library is the first stop — books may be borrowed; some books are required reading and may need personal copies. Specific textbook requirements come from your instructor's syllabus. Uniform requirements and the accredited suppliers vary per program and campus.
Student stores, coops, and outside-gate tailors supply CvSU students with school supplies and uniforms. Textbook requirements come per syllabus — start at the library.
May student store o coop, at mga tailor sa labas ng gate ng CvSU para sa school supplies at uniform. Para sa textbooks, magsimula sa library — ang specific requirements ay nasa syllabus.

CvSU enforces a basic dress code consistent with a professional learning environment — typically prohibiting overly revealing clothing, slippers in classrooms, and similar inappropriate attire. Specific departments may have stricter standards (e.g., lab gowns, closed shoes in engineering labs, PE attire, formal attire during department-specific activities). For everyday dress code rules and any campus-specific provisions, refer to the Student Handbook at your campus.

CvSU's dress code expects clothing appropriate to a professional learning environment. Some departments (engineering labs, PE, hospital practicum) have stricter requirements. See your Student Handbook.
May basic dress code ang CvSU na angkop sa propesyonal na pag-aaral. May mga departamento (engineering lab, PE, klinikal) na mas mahigpit. Tingnan ang Student Handbook.

Please know you are not alone, and what you're feeling matters. If you are in immediate danger or thinking of hurting yourself, please reach out right now:

- NCMH Crisis Line (24/7, toll-free landline in the Philippines): 1553
- Hopeline Philippines: (02) 8804-4673 / 0917 558-4673 / 0918 873-4673
- In life-threatening emergencies, call 911.

If you can, also reach out to someone you trust — a family member, friend, faculty member, or your campus Guidance and Counseling Office during office hours. CvSU's Guidance Office can connect you with longer-term support.

You matter, and there are people ready to help right now.

Please reach out right now if you're in crisis:
- NCMH Crisis Line (24/7, toll-free landline): 1553
- Hopeline Philippines: (02) 8804-4673 / 0917 558-4673
- In emergencies, call 911.
You can also contact your campus Guidance Office for ongoing support. You matter — please reach out.

Mahalaga ang iyong buhay. Kung nasa krisis ka, mangyaring tumawag agad:
- NCMH Crisis Line (24/7, toll-free): 1553
- Hopeline Philippines: (02) 8804-4673 / 0917 558-4673
- Sa emergency, tumawag ng 911.
Maaari ka ring sumangguni sa Guidance Office ng iyong campus para sa patuloy na suporta. Hindi ka nag-iisa.

Your official CvSU email account (typically on the cvsu.edu.ph domain) is issued by the ICT/MIS Office, usually during your first enrollment. This account is your channel for official notifications, learning-management-system access, and other digital services CvSU subscribes to. To activate, reset a password, or report problems, contact the ICT/MIS Office at your campus with your student ID. Avoid sharing the password or using the account for personal sign-ups — it's tied to university policies.

CvSU's official student email is on cvsu.edu.ph, issued by ICT/MIS at enrollment. Use it for official notifications and university services. For activation/password resets, contact your campus ICT/MIS Office.
Ang opisyal na CvSU email sa cvsu.edu.ph ay ibinibigay ng ICT/MIS sa enrollment. Gamitin ito para sa official notifications. Para sa activation/password reset, makipag-ugnayan sa ICT/MIS.

CvSU instructors deliver online and hybrid components of their courses through various platforms — commonly Google Classroom, Microsoft Teams, Zoom, and (where deployed) the university's learning-management system. Which platform you'll use depends on your instructor. Your CvSU email account is typically used to access these platforms. For platform-specific access issues or account problems, contact your instructor first, then ICT/MIS if it's an account/permissions issue.

Online/hybrid classes at CvSU use various platforms (Google Classroom, MS Teams, Zoom, the campus LMS). Your CvSU email logs you in. For access issues, ask your instructor; for account problems, ICT/MIS.
Ang mga online/hybrid class sa CvSU ay gumagamit ng iba't ibang platform (Google Classroom, MS Teams, Zoom, LMS). Ang CvSU email ay nagiging login. Magtanong sa instructor o ICT/MIS.

CvSU students may have access to Microsoft 365 (Office apps + cloud storage) and/or Google Workspace for Education through the university's institutional subscription, sign-in tied to your @cvsu.edu.ph account. Additional educational licenses (Adobe, Autodesk, etc.) may be offered by specific colleges (e.g., engineering, design programs) for in-classroom use. Specific entitlements and how to claim them vary — contact your campus ICT/MIS Office or your College for the current list.

Microsoft 365 and Google Workspace for Education may be accessible via your @cvsu.edu.ph account. Additional educational software (Adobe, Autodesk, etc.) may come through specific colleges. Check with ICT/MIS and your College.

Maaaring may access ka sa Microsoft 365 at Google Workspace for Education gamit ang iyong @cvsu.edu.ph account. Ang ibang educational software (Adobe, Autodesk) ay maaaring mula sa iyong college. Magtanong sa ICT/MIS.

CvSU graduates of board-program courses (Education, Engineering, Accountancy, Criminology, Nursing, Veterinary Medicine, etc.) take their licensure examinations under the Professional Regulation Commission (PRC). Some colleges offer in-house review classes or partner with accredited review centers; others let graduates choose external review centers. Board pass rates and review schedules vary per program and per academic year. For your college's review program (if offered) and tips for the relevant board exam, contact your Department Chair and check your alumni network.
Board exam reviews at CvSU vary by program — some colleges offer in-house review; others partner with external review centers. Verify with your Department Chair after graduation.
Ang board exam review sa CvSU ay nag-iiba bawat programa — may in-house, may external partner. Magtanong sa inyong Department Chair pagkatapos ng graduation.

If you plan to pursue graduate studies abroad after CvSU, the typical preparation includes: (1) good academic standing and an authenticated TOR (CAV + DFA apostille), (2) standardized exams as required by your target country/program (TOEFL/IELTS for English; GRE/GMAT for many graduate programs in the US), (3) recommendation letters from your CvSU professors, (4) a strong statement of purpose, and (5) scholarship application materials (Fulbright, DAAD, Chevening, Australia Awards, Erasmus, country-specific scholarships, etc.). Start research and document preparation 12–18 months ahead of your target intake.

For abroad graduate studies: authenticated TOR (CAV + DFA apostille), standardized exams (TOEFL/IELTS, possibly GRE/GMAT), recommendation letters, statement of purpose, and scholarship apps (Fulbright, DAAD, Chevening, Australia Awards, Erasmus). Plan 12–18 months ahead.

Para sa graduate studies sa ibang bansa pagkatapos ng CvSU: authenticated TOR (CAV + DFA apostille), standardized exams (TOEFL/IELTS, GRE/GMAT), recommendation letters, statement of purpose, at scholarship applications. Maghanda nang 12–18 buwan bago ang target.

For verification of your CvSU studies or graduation for an employer, you'll typically request a Certification of Enrollment or Graduation from the Registrar. For a personal recommendation letter, approach a professor or adviser who knows your work — give them at least 1–2 weeks' notice, an updated CV, and a brief on the role/program you're applying to. For Certificate of Internship Completion, your department's OJT coordinator issues this after you complete the OJT requirements. Specific fees and turnaround vary by document and campus.

Employment-related certifications: Registrar issues Certificates of Enrollment/Graduation; OJT coordinator issues Internship Completion; professors issue recommendation letters (give 1–2 weeks' notice + CV).

Para sa employment-related certifications: ang Registrar ang nagbibigay ng Certificate of Enrollment/Graduation; ang OJT coordinator ang nagbibigay ng Internship Completion; ang propesor naman ang nagbibigay ng recommendation letter (bigyan ng 1–2 linggo + CV).

Class suspensions at CvSU follow the LGU's announcement (governor or municipal mayor) and/or the university's official announcement during weather events, calamities, brownouts, or other emergencies. The Office of the President/Campus Administrator releases the official suspension notice through CvSU's verified Facebook page and other official channels. Do NOT rely on unofficial posts — check the verified CvSU pages or contact your College directly. In life-threatening conditions, prioritize safety regardless of an official notice.
Class suspensions follow LGU and/or CvSU official announcements via the verified Facebook page and official channels. Don't rely on unverified posts — check official pages or contact your College.
Ang class suspension sa CvSU ay sumusunod sa LGU at/o sa opisyal na anunsiyo ng CvSU sa verified Facebook page. Huwag umasa sa hindi opisyal na post — tingnan ang official channels.

patterns: sino ang tatawagan kapag may aksidente
responses:
For on-campus emergencies at CvSU:
- Life-threatening emergencies (medical, fire, crime): call 911 (national emergency number) immediately, then notify campus Security and the Health Service Unit.
- Minor medical concerns: go to the Health Service Unit (clinic) at your campus.
- Fire: activate the nearest fire alarm if available, evacuate via marked routes, do not use elevators, and gather at the assembly point.
- Earthquake: drop, cover, hold; evacuate after shaking stops via stairs to the assembly point.
Campus Security desks have direct contact lines to local emergency responders. Save your campus Security and Clinic numbers from the official directory.

On-campus emergency steps: 911 for life-threatening situations, Health Service Unit for minor medical issues, follow fire/earthquake protocols (alarm, evacuate via stairs, assemble at marked point). Save campus Security and Clinic contacts.

Para sa emergency sa CvSU campus: tumawag ng 911 para sa malubha, pumunta sa Health Service Unit para sa hindi mabigat na medical issue, sumunod sa fire/earthquake protocols. I-save ang Security at Clinic numbers ng inyong campus.

If you lost an item on campus, check with Campus Security first — most CvSU campuses maintain a Lost & Found at the Security Office or OSAS. Describe the item, where and approximately when you lost it, and leave your contact details so they can reach you if it turns up. For stolen items, file a report with Campus Security; serious incidents may also be reported to the local PNP station. For lost IDs, see the ID replacement intent.
Lost items: check with Campus Security/OSAS — they maintain Lost & Found. For stolen items, file a report with Security; serious cases also go to local PNP. For lost IDs, request replacement.
Para sa nawalan: tanungin ang Campus Security/OSAS — sila ang may Lost & Found. Para sa ninakaw, mag-file ng report sa Security; sa malulubha, sa lokal na PNP.

I won't make a 'better than' comparison between schools — every university has its strengths, and the right fit depends on your program, finances, distance, learning style, and goals. What I can say about CvSU: it is a Philippine state university covered by RA 10931 (free tuition for qualified Filipino undergraduate students), located in Cavite with a Main Campus in Indang and additional campuses across the province, with a wide range of programs in agriculture, engineering and IT, education, business, arts and sciences, and others. For verified specifics — accreditations, board pass rates, employment outcomes — please check https://cvsu.edu.ph and CHED's published data.

I won't rank CvSU against other schools — fit depends on your program, finances, distance, and goals. CvSU is a Philippine SUC, covered by RA 10931, with a main campus in Indang and additional campuses, offering a wide range of programs. Check https://cvsu.edu.ph for verified specifics.

Hindi ko ikinukumpara ang CvSU sa ibang paaralan — depende sa programa, pinansyal, distansya, at layunin ang tamang piliin. Ang CvSU ay isang state university (SUC) na sakop ng RA 10931, nasa Cavite, at may iba't ibang programa. Tingnan ang https://cvsu.edu.ph para sa detalye.

patterns: which course is best at CvSU
I don't know what to take
responses: Choosing a program is a personal decision — the 'best' course depends on your interests, strengths, financial situation, career goals, and the demand in your field. A useful approach: (1) list your interests and what energizes you to study, (2) check what programs CvSU offers that match, (3) look at the curriculum and the kind of work alumni do, (4) factor in your SHS strand and entrance-exam readiness, (5) consider career outcomes (board exam, employability). CvSU's Guidance Office offers career counseling — they can help you think this through.

Choosing a program depends on your interests, strengths, finances, and career goals. Check the curriculum, talk to alumni, factor in your SHS strand, and consider career outcomes. CvSU's Guidance Office offers career counseling.

Ang pagpili ng kurso ay personal — depende sa interes, kakayahan, pinansyal, at career goals. Tingnan ang curriculum, kausapin ang alumni, isaalang-alang ang SHS strand mo, at career outcomes. May career counseling sa Guidance Office.
